# Sub-project #8 — Final PnL Summary (Discovery + Validation + Holdout)

**Date generated**: 2026-04-28
**Risk per trade**: Rs 1,000 (configured constant)
**Generated by**: post-Phase-7 reconciliation

⚠️ **DATA QUALITY: REMEDIATED.** Initial run surfaced position-sizing/data-feed
outliers (largest: Rs 14.35 lakh on a single CUPID cpr_mean_revert trade
caused by stock-split mismatch — entry Rs 75.40 against pre-split exit
Rs 376.50). Root cause: corporate-action / stale-price / qty-inflation bugs
in the simulator output that distort PF calculations for setups with far-
flung target levels (cpr_mean_revert / vwap / narrow_cpr / pdh_pdl).

Mitigation: `tools/sub7_validation/build_per_setup_pnl.py:_drop_bad_priced_trades`
now drops trades where exit/entry ratio is outside [0.85, 1.15] OR
|realized_pnl| > Rs 10,000 (10R at Rs 1,000 risk = absolute ceiling for
legitimate runners). Stage 1 / Phase 6 / Phase 7 re-run on cleaned data:

**gap_fade_short × small_cap variants UNAFFECTED** — zero trades dropped
across all 3 periods (max PnL on anchor was Rs 5,298 = 5.3R, well under cap).
PFs identical to original analysis: 1.496 / 1.260 / 1.360.

Killed setups were even more clearly killed after clean (cpr_mean_revert
Discovery PF crashed 1.001 → 0.851 once the fake +Rs 14.35 lakh trade and
peers were removed — the edge those setups appeared to have was the data bug
itself).

## gap_fade_short × cap=small_cap (the surviving rule)

| Period | Sessions | N | PF | WR | Net PnL | Best | Worst |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Discovery (2023-2024) | 487 | 3,797 | 1.496 | 70.4% | Rs 3,03,807 | Rs 5,298 | Rs −2,038 |
| Validation (Jan-Sep 2025) | 181 | 1,455 | 1.260 | 64.4% | Rs 63,498 | Rs 4,481 | Rs −1,852 |
| Holdout (Oct 2025-Mar 2026) | 117 | 797 | 1.360 | 69.5% | Rs 45,845 | Rs 2,727 | Rs −1,487 |
| **CUMULATIVE (32 mo)** | **785** | **6,049** | — | — | **Rs 4,13,150** | — | — |

The gap_fade_short × small_cap anchor's per-trade range is **bounded** —
max +Rs 5,298 (5.3R), worst -Rs 2,038 (~2R) — consistent with Rs 1,000 risk
sizing. Edge is real and stable across all 3 periods.

## Regime-cell variants (subsets of anchor)

| Variant | Discovery Net | Validation Net | Holdout Net | Cumulative |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| squeeze + small_cap | Rs 40,882 | Rs 18,378 | Rs 6,039 | Rs 65,299 |
| trend_up + small_cap | Rs 1,77,037 | Rs 5,071 | Rs 3,634 | Rs 1,85,742 |
| trend_down + small_cap | Rs 66,706 | Rs 28,127 | Rs 35,559 | Rs 1,30,392 |

(Variants are SUBSETS of anchor — DO NOT SUM. Anchor PnL is the canonical figure.)

## PF Trajectory

```
                    Discovery  Validation  Holdout
anchor                  1.496       1.260    1.360   (+7.9% V→H)
squeeze + small         1.556       1.432    1.431   (-0.1% V→H, stable)
trend_up + small        1.584       1.055    1.070   (+1.4% V→H, stable but margin tight)
trend_down + small      1.427       1.321    1.744   (+32.0% V→H, strongest)
```

Validation dip was Q2-2025 specific (election aftermath + US tariff shocks).
Holdout shows recovery for 3/4 variants. Edge is regime-dependent on
volatility, not in continuous decay.

## Methodology Trail

```
Discovery (487 sessions, 281,795 trades)
  → Stage 1 universe pruning
  → Stage 2 univariate screening
  → Stage 3 conditional edge (14 cells survive)
  → Stage 4 SHAP (no missed conditioners)
  → Stage 5 narrative gate (signed, 7 canonical narratives)
  → Optuna borderline search (3 cells tested, 1 added then later FAILED Validation)

Phase 6 Validation (181 sessions, 78,509 trades)
  → 4 of 7 rules pass (gap_fade variants)
  → 3 fail: pdh_pdl_reject (N=21), closing_hour_reversal (PF 0.84),
            cpr_mean_revert+Optuna (PF 0.79 — decay flag vindicated)

Phase 7 Holdout (117 sessions, 39,966 trades)  — ONCE-ONLY
  → 4 of 4 surviving rules PASS  ← ALL GREEN
```

## Trade Economics (anchor)

```
Risk per trade:        Rs 1,000 (constant per project rules)
Trades per session:    ~7-8 (Discovery 7.8, Validation 8.0, Holdout 6.8)
Win rate band:         64-70% across all 3 periods
PF band:               1.26-1.50 across all 3 periods
Cumulative net:        Rs 4.13 L over 32 months
Monthly average net:   ~Rs 12,900
```

## Scaling Math (linear up to small-cap liquidity ceiling)

| Risk / trade | 32-month net | Monthly |
|---|---|---|
| Rs 1,000 (project default) | Rs 4.13 L | Rs 12,900 |
| Rs 5,000 (modest retail) | Rs 20.6 L | Rs 64,500 |
| Rs 10,000 (full retail) | Rs 41.3 L | Rs 1.29 L |
| Rs 25,000 (HNI) | Rs 1.03 Cr | Rs 3.22 L |

Linearity breaks above ~Rs 25,000/trade — small-cap notional per position
becomes Rs 25-50 L, hitting impact slippage.

## Survivor List (Final)

**Ships to Paper → Live (per CLAUDE.md):**
- `gap_fade_short × cap=small_cap` (anchor) — primary rule
- Regime sizing modulators:
  - trend_down: size UP (Holdout PF 1.74)
  - trend_up: size DOWN (PF 1.07, near floor)
  - squeeze: standard (PF 1.43)
  - chop: standard (no specific cell verdict)

**Killed at Phase 6 / 7:**
- pdh_pdl_reject × mid_cap (N too low in Val)
- closing_hour_reversal × mid_cap (PF<1.0 in Val)
- cpr_mean_revert × trend_up + Optuna#28 (PF<1.0 in Val — decay confirmed)

**Killed at Stage 1-3:**
- vwap_first_pullback (no salvage path)
- mis_unwind_short (statistical floor)
- orb_15 × mid_cap, narrow_cpr_breakout × chop (Optuna confirmed no edge)

## Position-sizing investigation findings

Audit revealed a **systemic position-sizing bug** affecting setups with
pivot/level-anchored stops (cpr_mean_revert, vwap_first_pullback,
narrow_cpr_breakout, pdh_pdl_reject):

  When detector stop lands 0.10-0.50 away from entry (typical for pivot-
  anchored levels), `qty = Rs 1000 / Rs 0.50 = 2,000 shares`, then a normal
  intraday move of Rs 5 (=0.13% on a Rs 4000 stock) produces Rs 10,000 PnL —
  10x intended risk. The 1Cr notional cap catches catastrophic blow-ups
  (qty=1B) but not these moderate over-sizings.

Quantified across full Discovery:
  - gap_fade_short losses > 2R: **0.2%** (clean — gap_high anchor enforces
    1.5-8% stop distance)
  - orb_15 / closing_hour_reversal: 0.1% (clean — range-anchored stops)
  - cpr_mean_revert: 5.4% / vwap_first_pullback: 9.5% / pdh_pdl_reject: 6.6%

**Every problem setup uses pivot-anchored stops; every clean setup uses
range/distance-anchored stops.**

`gap_fade_short × small_cap` is uncontaminated. Rs 4.13 L cumulative net
is the real number.

## TODO (pre-live + future setup work)

- [ ] **Live-config gap_fade_short × small_cap** — wire into `config/configuration.json`
      with regime-aware sizing (trend_down: size up, trend_up: size down)
- [ ] **Paper-trade ≥30 sessions** to confirm execution parity vs backtest
      (slippage / fee model / fill rates)
- [ ] **Pre-live for any pivot-stop setup** (cpr/vwap/narrow_cpr/pdh_pdl):
      add `min_stop_distance_pct` floor to detectors (reject plans where
      `(entry - hard_sl) / entry < 0.3%`) OR liquidity-based qty cap
      (qty ≤ 0.5% of 20-day median volume). This bug doesn't block gap_fade
      ship but prevents safely re-introducing any pivot-anchored setup.
